events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need parse scenario: manufacturing/assembly floor, Automotive & Powertrain, Fluids & leaks, observed field condition: "milkshake oil cap (FD)". Work rough-complete. Need identify component/condition and visible defect cues. "milkshake oil cap" means oil cap with milky emulsion indicating coolant mixing with oil, likely head gasket failure or condensation? In production floor, oil cap has milky residue. FD maybe "found during"? Need component condition installed-defective. Decision fail because non-compliant/dangerous? It's a defect indicating coolant in oil, engine internal leak, severe. Risk high/critical. Need findings: milky emulsion under oil cap, coolant contamination of engine oil, possible head gasket leak/cracked component. Actions: isolate vehicle/engine, inspect cooling system and engine oil, perform coolant pressure test/oil analysis, replace defective gasket/component, flush oil, verify no leaks, document quality gate failure. References: OEM specs, maybe SAE J standards? Since only OEM specs reference, use "OEM engine oil and coolant contamination specifications", "OEM quality gate fluid leak standards". Need fill all fields. percent_complete maybe 80? rough-complete. value 0. sound_source etc default empty string? Schema says string for sound_source, event_time numeric, rate numeric, order list, workable list. Use empty string, 0.0, [].

Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

events.label: response
events.text:

  "findings": [
    "Oil filler cap coated with thick milky/tan emulsion ('milkshake')",
    "Evidence of coolant mixing with engine oil",
    "Indication of internal engine coolant leak or combustion-gas intrusion",
    "Potential head gasket, cylinder head, or block defect",
    "Oil contamination visible at a quality gate before close-out"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Quarantine the engine/vehicle at the quality gate",
    "Do not start or run the engine until cause is confirmed",
    "Drain and inspect engine oil for coolant contamination",
    "Perform cooling-system pressure test and combustion-gas leak test per OEM procedure",
    "Inspect cylinder head gasket, head, and block for leaks or cracks",
    "Replace defective gasket or component as required",
    "Flush contaminated oil system and refill with specified oil",
    "Re-inspect oil cap and oil condition before release",
    "Document the defect and corrective action in the quality record"
  ],
  "rationale": "A 'milkshake' oil cap indicates coolant has emulsified with engine oil, signaling an internal leak or defective sealing component. This is a critical fluid-contamination defect in an Automotive & Powertrain assembly quality gate and must fail the inspection.",
  "references": [
    "OEM engine oil and coolant contamination limits",
    "OEM cylinder head gasket and cooling-system leak-test procedures",
    "OEM assembly quality gate fluid-leak standards"
  ]
